other_names: Arrow toggle, Collapse, Collapsible sections, Disclosure, Expandable, ShowyHideyThing
description: A vertical stack of headings used to toggle the display of further information.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-expanded to indicate expanded or collapsed state; ensure headings are focusable and keyboard-accessible.

description: Displays important information or status changes in a prominent way.
accessibility_considerations: Should be announced by screen readers (use role="alert" or role="status"); ensure it does not disappear too quickly.

description: A graphical representation of a user (photo, illustration, initials).
accessibility_considerations: Use alt text to describe the image for screen readers.

description: A small label representing a status or metadata.
accessibility_considerations: Use appropriate ARIA labels to describe its purpose, especially if it conveys important info.

description: Shows the user's location within a site's navigational hierarchy.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-current to indicate the current page; make links accessible with semantic nav elements.

description: Triggers actions (e.g., submit, show/hide).
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-pressed for toggle buttons; ensure buttons are accessible by keyboard.

description: Groups multiple, related buttons.
accessibility_considerations: Use role="group" for screen readers.

description: A container for a single entity, such as a contact or article.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-labelledby and aria-describedby to clarify relationships.

description: Binary input for selecting options, alone or in groups.
accessibility_considerations: Ensure labels are clearly associated with inputs using for attributes.

description: Input for choosing a color.
accessibility_considerations: Provide a text alternative for screen readers; ensure keyboard access.

description: Allows users to select a date or range of dates from a calendar interface.
accessibility_considerations: Ensure keyboard navigation is possible for date selection; provide a text alternative or use aria-live for dynamically updated information.

description: A window that appears over the main content, usually requiring user interaction before closing.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-modal to indicate its modal nature; focus should remain within the dialog while it is open.

description: A list of options that expands vertically when clicked or tapped.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-expanded to indicate expanded/collapsed state; ensure each option is focusable by keyboard.

description: Allows users to upload files from their device.
accessibility_considerations: Ensure a clear label and instructions for users; announce the upload status.

description: A control for collecting user input, typically within forms.
accessibility_considerations: Use appropriate labels and aria-required for mandatory fields; provide error feedback clearly.

description: A small graphic element often used to symbolize an action or status.
accessibility_considerations: Provide descriptive aria-label or aria-hidden if decorative; ensure contrast against background.

description: A structured group of items, such as text, images, or links.
accessibility_considerations: Use semantic list elements (e.g., <ul>, <ol>); ensure focusable items.

description: A set of options or links, usually in a collapsible or pop-up style for navigation or action selection.
accessibility_considerations: Ensure keyboard accessibility and use role="menu" with appropriate aria attributes for structure.

description: Navigational control for dividing content across multiple pages.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-labels for navigation controls, announce the current page, and ensure keyboard accessibility.

description: Shows the status or completion of a process, such as loading or uploading.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-live for updates, ensure screen readers convey the progress, and use aria-valuenow for specific percentages.

description: A set of options where only one choice is selectable.
accessibility_considerations: Ensure the radio group has a label and that each option is associated with its group.

description: Allows users to select a value from a range by sliding a handle.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow for screen reader support. Ensure keyboard accessibility.

description: Organizes data in rows and columns for easy comparison and sorting.
accessibility_considerations: Use appropriate header cells (<th>) with aria-sort for sortable tables; ensure captions and summaries for context.

description: Organizes content into labeled sections, where only one section is visible at a time.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-selected for active tabs; ensure tab panel is focusable.

description: A temporary, non-intrusive message that appears briefly to communicate updates or feedback.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-live to announce the message to screen readers; ensure it is not dismissible without keyboard access.

description: Guides users through a sequence of steps to complete a process, such as a form wizard.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-current for the active step, and ensure screen readers can announce the progress and each step's label.

description: Provides contextual information when users hover, focus, or tap on an element.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-describedby to associate the tooltip with its trigger; ensure tooltips are keyboard-accessible and dismissible by keyboard.

description: Allows users to toggle between two states, often resembling a physical switch.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-checked to indicate state, ensure clear on/off labels, and provide keyboard operability.

description: A table that displays large data sets with features like sorting, filtering, and pagination.
accessibility_considerations: Use appropriate headers and aria-sort for sortable columns, and make row and cell focus states accessible.

description: A brief message that appears at the bottom of the screen to provide feedback on an action.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-live for announcements, and ensure it stays visible long enough for all users to read.

description: A compact control for switching between options, commonly seen in mobile interfaces.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-pressed for selected segments, ensure each segment is focusable by keyboard.

description: A section for filtering data, commonly with multiple selectable filters.
accessibility_considerations: Ensure each filter is labeled clearly and keyboard-accessible; use aria-controls to connect filters to the affected content.

description: A rotating set of content, such as images or cards, that users can cycle through.
accessibility_considerations: Provide controls to start, stop, and navigate between slides; use aria-live for changes and keyboard navigation.

description: Allows users to select a rating, often represented by stars or another graphical representation.
accessibility_considerations: Use aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax for ratings, and ensure all ratings are keyboard-accessible.

description: Input field for users to enter search terms.
accessibility_considerations: Use clear labeling for the search field; implement aria-live for search suggestions or dynamic search results.

description: A visual representation of a status, often using color and iconography (e.g., online/offline).
accessibility_considerations: Use text labels and ARIA roles to convey status, and ensure color contrast meets accessibility standards.

description: Lists sections of a document or page, allowing users to jump to different parts.
accessibility_considerations: Use appropriate list semantics, ensure keyboard accessibility, and highlight the current section.

description: Displays a hierarchical list, often collapsible, like a file structure.
accessibility_considerations: Use appropriate ARIA roles and properties to convey hierarchy, and ensure keyboard navigation.
